sectionHeadings: 021: Release supply-chain artifacts (per-target SBOM and build provenance)
record.summary: Every prebuilt binary spec-spine ships should carry a machine-readable bill of materials and a verifiable provenance trail. This spec adds, to the tag-gated release pipeline (007's release.yml), a per-target CycloneDX SBOM generated by syft for each of the five release archives, plus a SLSA build-provenance attestation over each archive. The SBOM names the dependency graph baked into that binary (read from the committed Cargo.lock, so it is as deterministic as the build); a fail-closed check refuses to ship an SBOM with zero components. SBOMs are attached to the GitHub Release as separate assets named to match their archive; the provenance attestation is recorded in GitHub's attestation store so any archive verifies with `gh attestation verify`. This is a port of OAP spec 117, scaled to spec-spine's single-CLI, five-triple release. The channels themselves (crates.io, npm, PyPI) are unchanged; this is purely additive supply-chain metadata on the binary release.
record.title: Release supply-chain artifacts: per-target SBOM and build provenance
